ASBESTOS ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jul-30-2020 Off Calendar

ASBESTOS ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Feb-06-2020 Continued To Aug-06-20 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 503

Notice Of Motion For Order To Apportion And Distribute Funds
Off Calendar Nov-26-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for hearing on the Motion for an Order to Apportion and Distribute Settlement Funds.
Dept. 514. Judge: Gail Dekreon. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty. Reporter: Patricia Dowling, CSR #5388. Email: pdowling@sftc.org.

Janine M. Fiel-Cosse, Esq. appeared for plaintiffs Barbara Schaffer, as Successor-in-Interest to William Reid, Deceased, and Wrongful Death Heirs Carla Guzman, Jeffrey Reid and Debra Reid, who were not present for this morning's proceedings.

The Court, having reviewed the moving papers and having considered the evidence, both oral and documentary, and good cause appearing, RULES that the following are entitled to distribution of all net funds obtained by their counsel, Brayton Purcell LLP, devived from all funds (both litigation furnds and funds from claims against bankrupt abestos defendant trusts) negotiated after the July 13, 2016 death of William Reid in the following proportions:

Estate of William Reid, deceased - 100%.
Carla Guzman as Wrongful Death Heir - 0%.
Jeffrey Reid as Wrongful Death Heir - 0.%.
Debra Reid as Wrongful Death Heir - 0%.

Additionally, funds shall be issued by Brayton Purcell LLP in the percentages set forth above, subject first to fees and costs (if any outstanding), and liens, including but not limited to liens (if any) payable to the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services' Medicare program and the California Department of Health Care Services Medi-Cal Program, and subject to any holds placed on funds awaiting response from such agencies.

All future net settlement funds derived from settlements entered into after the July 13, 2016 death of William Reid shall be distributed in the percentages set forth above.

The signature of Barbara Schaffer shall be sufficient on all settlement documents for the purpose of settling both the estate claim and the wrongful death claims with defendants in the filed asbestos personal injury - survival and wrongful death action and in those claims made agains bankrupt-related trusts established for abestos victims.

Proposed Order to Apportion and Distribute Funds was submitted to and signed by the Court.
